title: 95 By BP
description: This dataset comprises EEG neuroimaging recordings from 95 participants who received individualized laser pain stimulation at varying intensities calibrated to their pain thresholds. Participants rated perceived pain intensity on a numerical rating scale (0-10) across four stimulation levels (NRS ratings of 2, 4, 6, and 8) applied to bilateral hand dorsum locations. The study employs a within-subject repeated measures design with 80 total trials per participant, providing a comprehensive resource for investigating neural correlates of pain perception and individual differences in pain sensitivity.
methods_description: Laser stimulation intensity was individually calibrated based on each participant's pain threshold, determined through ascending stimulus series in 0.25J increments. Four standardized stimulation intensities were used (E1: 2.0±0.2J, E2: 2.7±0.3J, E3: 3.4±0.3J, E4: 4.1±0.4J) corresponding to NRS ratings of 2, 4, 6, and 8. Stimulation was applied to left and right hand dorsum in a counterbalanced design with 10 trials per condition. Participants provided pain intensity ratings using an 11-point numerical rating scale (0=no sensation, 10=maximum tolerable pain) in a quiet, temperature-controlled environment. Both participants and experimenters wore protective goggles during stimulation.
